title: polygala vulgaris subsp. insubrica
intro: Polygala vulgaris subsp. insubrica is a subspecies of the common polygala, a flowering plant belonging to the milkwort family (Polygalaceae). It is native to the Alps and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiviral and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is used to support immune function, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and mucolytic properties. Herbal preparations of Polygala vulgaris subsp. insubrica include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal benefits.
